combat_history.event: Juan Carlos I deployed to the Philippines for humanitarian assistance following Typhoon Haiyan, demonstrating long-range deployment capability and disaster relief role.
combat_history.significance: First major overseas deployment, proved the ship's strategic mobility and humanitarian mission capability

combat_history.event: Multiple deployments to Eastern Mediterranean as part of NATO's maritime security operation, conducting surveillance and maritime situational awareness missions.
combat_history.significance: Demonstrated integration with NATO operations and sustained deployment capability

combat_history.event: Used as floating hospital and logistics base during Spanish COVID-19 response, utilizing medical facilities and large internal spaces.
combat_history.significance: Showed versatility in non-traditional roles and value of large deck ships for national emergencies

modernization.description: Spanish Navy studying potential integration of F-35B Lightning II to replace aging AV-8B Harrier fleet. Would require modifications to flight deck and support systems.
modernization.impact: Would significantly enhance strike capability and interoperability with NATO F-35 operators

modernization.description: Upgrading SCOMBA combat management system and INDRA radar systems for improved air defense and situational awareness.
modernization.impact: Enhanced self-defense capability and command and control for amphibious operations

modernization.description: Installation of new SATCOM systems and Link 16 capability for improved NATO interoperability.
modernization.impact: Better integration with allied forces and enhanced command capabilities

vulnerabilities.description: Limited to point defense systems with no medium or long-range SAM capability. Relies entirely on escort vessels or friendly air cover for area air defense.
vulnerabilities.context: In contested environments against near-peer threats, the ship would be vulnerable to coordinated air or missile attacks
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Spanish Navy typically operates with F-100 frigates providing area air defense, but this limits operational flexibility

vulnerabilities.description: As Spain's only large deck amphibious vessel, any major damage or maintenance issues eliminate the entire capability. No backup platform exists.
vulnerabilities.context: Critical vulnerability for Spanish power projection and NATO commitments
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Spanish Navy is considering second LHD but budget constraints remain significant

vulnerabilities.description: Current AV-8B Harrier fleet is aging with no immediate replacement confirmed. Limited hangar space constrains mixed aviation operations.
vulnerabilities.context: Reduces strike capability and operational effectiveness compared to modern F-35B equipped platforms
vulnerabilities.mitigation: F-35B procurement under consideration but not confirmed due to cost and political factors

vulnerabilities.description: High deployment rate for single platform leads to maintenance stress and crew fatigue. Ship has operated at high tempo since commissioning.
vulnerabilities.context: May impact long-term material condition and crew readiness for high-intensity operations
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Limited by lack of sister ship; Spanish Navy managing through extended maintenance periods

operational_patterns.typical_deployment: Flagship for Spanish amphibious task groups, NATO exercises, humanitarian operations, and sovereignty missions in Spanish territories
operational_patterns.typical_task_group: Usually operates with F-100 class frigate, replenishment ship, and submarine escort for major deployments
operational_patterns.readiness_notes: High operational tempo with frequent deployments limits availability for maintenance. Single platform creates scheduling pressures.

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Mistral has larger well deck and troop capacity but smaller flight deck and no ski-jump for fixed-wing operations. Juan Carlos I prioritizes aviation over amphibious capacity.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: European LHD face-off: Aviation vs amphibious capacity trade-offs in medium navy power projection

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Trieste is newer with more advanced systems and F-35B integration from the start. Juan Carlos I larger but older design with upgrade limitations.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Old vs new European carriers: First vs second generation LHD design philosophy

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Based on Juan Carlos I but modified for Australian requirements. No ski-jump initially, different radar and combat systems, enhanced command facilities.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Design evolution: How Australia improved the Spanish LHD design for Pacific operations

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Similar size and role but Izumo designed around helicopter operations initially, now converting for F-35B. Juan Carlos I built for fixed-wing from start.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: East vs West LHD concepts: Spanish multi-role vs Japanese helicopter destroyer evolution

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Type 075 larger with greater amphibious capacity but no fixed-wing capability. Represents different operational concept focused purely on amphibious assault.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: LHD philosophy clash: Chinese amphibious focus vs European multi-role flexibility
